1→# Data and State — Charlotte Collection Auction Platform
     2→
     3→*Phase 3 (WHAT) — Spec-Driven Development*
     4→*Version: v1*
     5→*Date: 2026-03-24*
     6→*Author: CTO Agent*
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1. Overview
    11→
    12→This document specifies every data structure, state machine, calculation formula, and event trigger in the Charlotte Collection auction platform. It is the companion to the site design spec (03a) — where 03a defines what the user sees, this document defines what the system knows.
    13→
    14→**What this document covers:**
    15→- Entity schemas (paintings, bidders, bids, rewards, the auction itself)
    16→- The auction state machine (painting-level and auction-level)
    17→- Calculation formulas (tier pricing, WTA price, threshold, reward disbursements)
    18→- Notification event triggers and routing
    19→- Content management schema (narratives, videos, series metadata)
    20→- Temporal rules (what changes over time, what is immutable)
    21→
    22→**What this document does NOT cover:**
    23→- Visual presentation → `spec-v1-03a-site-design.md`
    24→- Database engine, API contracts, infrastructure → `spec-v1-04-technical-architecture.md`
    25→
    26→**Conventions:**
    27→- Field types use generic notation: `string`, `integer`, `decimal`, `boolean`, `datetime`, `enum`, `text`, `url`, `uuid`
    28→- `decimal` means exact decimal arithmetic (not floating point) — financial calculations must never use floats
    29→- `datetime` is always stored in UTC with timezone offset
    30→- All monetary amounts are stored in **cents** (integer) to avoid floating-point precision issues. Display conversion to dollars happens at the presentation layer.
    31→- Required fields marked with `*`. Optional fields unmarked.
    32→- References use `→ Entity` notation
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 2. Entity: Painting
    37→
    38→A painting is the core product unit. There are exactly 52 paintings in the Charlotte Collection, defined before auction launch and immutable during the auction.
    39→
    40→### 2.1 Schema
    41→
    42→| Field | Type | Description |
    43→|---|---|---|
    44→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
    45→| `slug` * | string | URL-safe identifier derived from title (e.g., `acceptance`, `anger`). Immutable after creation. |
    46→| `title` * | string | Display title (e.g., "Acceptance") |
    47→| `width_inches` * | decimal | Width in inches |
    48→| `height_inches` * | decimal | Height in inches |
    49→| `square_inches` * | integer | Computed: `ceil(width_inches × height_inches)`. The pricing basis. |
    50→| `tier1_price_cents` * | integer | Computed: `square_inches × 100` ($1/sq.in. in cents) |
    51→| `tier2_price_cents` * | integer | Computed: `square_inches × 1000` ($10/sq.in. in cents) |
    52→| `tier3_price_cents` * | integer | Computed: `square_inches × 10000` ($100/sq.in. in cents) |
    53→| `tier4_price_cents` * | integer | Computed: `square_inches × 100000` ($1,000/sq.in. in cents). WTA-only. |
    54→| `display_order` * | integer | Artist-curated gallery position (1–52). Not alphabetical, not by size. |
    55→| `series_id` | → Series | Theme series membership (nullable — most paintings have no series) |
    56→| `series_order` | integer | Position within series (nullable) |
    57→| `size_category` * | enum | `small` (≤12" longest side), `medium` (12"–24"), `large` (24"–48"), `oversized` (>48") |
    58→| `shipping_estimate_low_cents` * | integer | Low end of shipping estimate for size category |
    59→| `shipping_estimate_high_cents` * | integer | High end of shipping estimate for size category |
    60→| `narrative` | text | Written description: story, emotion, context. 1–3 paragraphs by the artist. |
    61→| `video_url` | url | Embedded video URL (YouTube, Vimeo). Nullable — not all paintings have video. |
    62→| `image_source_url` * | url | Full-resolution original in Vercel Blob. Next.js Image component handles resizing/format conversion on demand (see 04 §13.2). |
    63→| `image_og_url` * | url | Pre-generated 1200×630px for Open Graph social previews. |
    64→| `alt_text` * | string | Descriptive alt text: "[Title], acrylic on canvas, [W]" × [H]"" |
    65→| `created_at` * | datetime | Record creation timestamp |
    66→
    67→### 2.2 Derived Display Values
    68→
    69→These are not stored — computed at the presentation layer:
    70→
    71→| Value | Formula |
    72→|---|---|
    73→| Width in cm | `width_inches × 2.54` |
    74→| Height in cm | `height_inches × 2.54` |
    75→| Dimensions display (US) | `{width}" × {height}"` or `{width}' × {height}'` if ≥ 12" |
    76→| Dimensions display (metric) | `{width_cm} cm × {height_cm} cm` |
    77→
    78→### 2.3 Immutability Rules
    79→
    80→| Field | Mutable? | When |
    81→|---|---|---|
    82→| Physical attributes (dimensions, title, slug) | No | Set at catalog creation. Never changes. |
    83→| Content (narrative, video, images) | Yes | Updatable by the artist at any time, including during auction. |
    84→| Pricing (tier prices) | No | Derived from dimensions. Cannot be overridden. |
    85→| Display order | Yes | Artist can reorder gallery before launch. Frozen at auction open. |
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## 3. Entity: Series
    90→
    91→A thematic grouping of paintings. At launch, only the Grief Series exists.
    92→
    93→### 3.1 Schema
    94→
    95→| Field | Type | Description |
    96→|---|---|---|
    97→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
    98→| `slug` * | string | URL-safe identifier (e.g., `grief`) |
    99→| `title` * | string | Display title (e.g., "The Grief Series") |
   100→| `narrative` | text | Series story. Written by the artist. |
   101→| `display_order` * | integer | Order among series (for future expansion) |
   102→
   103→### 3.2 Relationships
   104→
   105→- A Series has many Paintings (via `painting.series_id`)
   106→- Paintings within a series are ordered by `painting.series_order`
   107→- Series membership has **no structural auction effect** — it is purely presentational (Key Decision, auction-mechanism.md §5.1)
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## 4. Entity: Bidder
   112→
   113→A registered user of the platform. Registration is required before bidding but not for browsing.
   114→
   115→### 4.1 Schema
   116→
   117→| Field | Type | Description |
   118→|---|---|---|
   119→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
   120→| `email` * | string | Verified email address. Primary communication channel. |
   121→| `full_legal_name` * | string | Required for settlement, certificates, tax documents |
   122→| `phone` * | string | Required for transaction-critical communications |
   123→| `mailing_address` * | object | Structured address (street, city, state, zip). US addresses only. Required for shipping and sales tax. |
   124→| `auth_provider` * | enum | `email`, `google`, `apple` |
   125→| `auth_provider_id` | string | OAuth provider's user ID (nullable for email auth) |
   126→| `password_hash` | string | Bcrypt hash (nullable — only for email auth) |
   127→| `email_verified` * | boolean | Must be `true` before bidding |
   128→| `notify_email` * | boolean | Always `true` — email notifications cannot be disabled |
   129→| `notify_sms` * | boolean | Default `false`. Bidder opt-in. |
   130→| `notify_push` * | boolean | Default `false`. Bidder opt-in. |
   131→| `terms_accepted_at` * | datetime | Timestamp of first terms acknowledgment (bid flow Screen 2) |
   132→| `terms_version` * | string | Version of terms accepted |
   133→| `created_at` * | datetime | Registration timestamp |
   134→| `updated_at` * | datetime | Last profile update |
   135→
   136→### 4.2 Mailing Address Schema
   137→
   138→| Field | Type | Description |
   139→|---|---|---|
   140→| `street1` * | string | Street address line 1 |
   141→| `street2` | string | Street address line 2 (apt, suite, etc.) |
   142→| `city` * | string | City |
   143→| `state` * | string | US state (two-letter code) |
   144→| `zip` * | string | ZIP code (5-digit or ZIP+4) |
   145→
   146→### 4.3 Constraints
   147→
   148→- US addresses only (Key Decision #5: US buyers only)
   149→- Email must be verified before any bid can be placed
   150→- One account per email address
   151→- Full legal name is immutable after first bid placement (used on legal documents)
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## 5. Entity: Bid
   156→
   157→A bid is a paid commitment to purchase a specific painting at a specific tier. Bids are irrevocable, paid at time of placement, and held in third-party escrow.
   158→
   159→### 5.1 Schema
   160→
   161→| Field | Type | Description |
   162→|---|---|---|
   163→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
   164→| `painting_id` * | → Painting | The painting being bid on |
   165→| `bidder_id` * | → Bidder | The person placing the bid |
   166→| `tier` * | enum | `tier1`, `tier2`, `tier3` |
   167→| `amount_cents` * | integer | Bid amount in cents. Equal to `painting.tier{N}_price_cents`. |
   168→| `status` * | enum | See §5.2 |
   169→| `visibility` * | enum | `public`, `private`. Default: `private`. Changeable by bidder. |
   170→| `display_name` | string | The bidder's name shown if `visibility = public`. Nullable if private. |
   171→| `payment_intent_id` * | string | Payment processor's transaction reference |
   172→| `escrow_reference` | string | Fiduciary's escrow tracking number (populated after escrow confirmation) |
   173→| `refund_id` | string | Payment processor's refund reference (populated on refund) |
   174→| `refund_initiated_at` | datetime | Timestamp of refund initiation |
   175→| `placed_at` * | datetime | Timestamp of successful payment and bid placement |
   176→| `outbid_at` | datetime | Timestamp when this bid was superseded by a higher tier |
   177→| `created_at` * | datetime | Record creation timestamp (may differ from `placed_at` due to payment processing time) |
   178→
   179→### 5.2 Bid Status Enum
   180→
   181→| Status | Meaning | Transitions From |
   182→|---|---|---|
   183→| `payment_processing` | Payment initiated, not yet confirmed | (initial) |
   184→| `active` | Payment confirmed, bid is the current holder for this painting/tier | `payment_processing` |
   185→| `outbid` | A higher-tier bid was placed. Refund initiated. | `active` |
   186→| `won` | Auction succeeded, this is the highest-tier bid on the painting | `active` |
   187→| `refunded_auction_failed` | Auction failed threshold check. Full refund. | `active` |
   188→| `refunded_wta` | WTA bid was placed. Refund initiated. | `active` |
   189→| `payment_failed` | Payment could not be processed | `payment_processing` |
   190→
   191→### 5.3 Constraints
   192→
   193→- **One active bid per tier per painting.** At most one bid with `status = active` may exist for a given (`painting_id`, `tier`) pair.
   194→- **One active bid per person per painting.** A bidder may not hold `active` bids at multiple tiers on the same painting.
   195→- **Sequential tier progression.** A `tier2` bid on a painting requires an existing `active` bid at `tier1` on that painting. A `tier3` bid requires an existing `active` bid at `tier2`. (See §8 Auction State Machine.)
   196→- **A bidder who was outbid may bid again** at the next available tier on the same painting. The outbid refund and the new bid are independent transactions.
   197→- **Self-outbidding is permitted.** A bidder holding `tier1` may place `tier2` on the same painting. The `tier1` bid transitions to `outbid`, the bidder is refunded, and they earn the tier1 outbid reward at settlement.
   198→- **Irrevocable.** No transition from `active` to any cancelled/withdrawn state. Only `outbid`, `won`, `refunded_auction_failed`, or `refunded_wta`.
   199→- **All bids are paid.** A bid cannot reach `active` status without confirmed payment.
   200→
   201→### 5.4 Payment Processing Window
   202→
   203→When a bidder initiates a bid (clicks "Place Bid"), a `payment_processing` record is created. This establishes a **soft hold** on the tier:
   204→
   205→- Other bidders see "Bid in progress..." on the tier button (03a §6.2)
   206→- The hold expires after a configurable timeout (recommended: 5 minutes)
   207→- If payment succeeds → transition to `active`
   208→- If payment fails or times out → transition to `payment_failed`, tier becomes available again
   209→- Only one `payment_processing` record may exist per (`painting_id`, `tier`) at a time
   210→
   211→### 5.5 Race Condition Resolution
   212→
   213→If two bidders click "Place Bid" on the same tier within milliseconds:
   214→
   215→1. The first to create a `payment_processing` record wins the hold
   216→2. The second receives an immediate response: "Bid in progress — another bidder is currently completing a transaction for this tier"
   217→3. If the first bidder's payment fails, the tier reopens and the second bidder can try again
   218→4. This is enforced by a database-level unique constraint or atomic lock on (`painting_id`, `tier`, `status = payment_processing OR active`)
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## 6. Entity: Reward
   223→
   224→A reward is the outbid bidder's 50% share of the tier spread. Rewards are calculated at settlement and paid from the winning bidder's escrowed funds.
   225→
   226→### 6.1 Schema
   227→
   228→| Field | Type | Description |
   229→|---|---|---|
   230→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
   231→| `painting_id` * | → Painting | The painting this reward relates to |
   232→| `recipient_id` * | → Bidder | The outbid bidder who earns this reward |
   233→| `lower_tier` * | enum | The tier the recipient held (`tier1`, `tier2`, or `tier3`) |
   234→| `upper_tier` * | enum | The tier that outbid them (`tier2`, `tier3`, or `tier4_wta`) |
   235→| `spread_cents` * | integer | `upper_tier_price - lower_tier_price` (in cents) |
   236→| `reward_cents` * | integer | `spread_cents / 2` (50% of spread — always exact because spread is always even in this pricing model) |
   237→| `source_bid_id` * | → Bid | The outbid bid that generated this reward |
   238→| `winning_bid_id` | → Bid | The bid that outbid the recipient (nullable for WTA — WTA is not a standard bid) |
   239→| `wta_bid_id` | → WTABid | If this reward was triggered by WTA (nullable) |
   240→| `status` * | enum | `pending`, `disbursed`, `cancelled` |
   241→| `disbursement_reference` | string | Fiduciary's payment reference (populated at disbursement) |
   242→| `disbursed_at` | datetime | Timestamp of reward payment |
   243→| `created_at` * | datetime | Record creation timestamp |
   244→
   245→### 6.2 Reward Calculation Rules
   246→
   247→Rewards are **not calculated at time of outbid** — they are calculated at settlement, after the auction succeeds. The reason: if the auction fails, no rewards are paid. Calculating rewards early would create misleading expectations.
   248→
   249→**At settlement, for each painting that has at least one outbid bid:**
   250→
   251→1. Identify the chain of bids: tier1 → tier2 → tier3 (or tier4 for WTA)
   252→2. For each tier transition, calculate:
   253→   - `spread = upper_tier_price - lower_tier_price`
   254→   - `reward = spread / 2`
   255→3. Create a Reward record for each transition
   256→
   257→**Example: Painting (3,456 sq.in.) sold at Tier 3 with full chain:**
   258→
   259→| Transition | Spread | Reward (to outbid bidder) | Artist share of spread |
   260→|---|---|---|---|
   261→| Tier 1 → Tier 2 | $31,104 | $15,552 | $15,552 |
   262→| Tier 2 → Tier 3 | $311,040 | $155,520 | $155,520 |
   263→| **Artist also receives base** | | | **$3,456** (Tier 1 equivalent) |
   264→
   265→### 6.3 Reward Precision Guarantee
   266→
   267→The tier prices are always `square_inches × {1, 10, 100, 1000}` dollars, stored in cents. The spread between any two adjacent tiers is always `square_inches × {9, 90, 900}` dollars. Dividing by 2 always yields an exact cent amount because `square_inches` is an integer and `{9, 90, 900}` are all odd multiples — but the product in cents is always even (since we multiply by 100 for cents conversion first). No rounding is ever needed.
   268→
   269→### 6.4 Self-Outbid Rewards
   270→
   271→When a bidder outbids themselves (§5.3), the reward recipient is the same person as the new bidder. This is intentional — they earn their own outbid reward, effectively reducing their net cost by ~50% of each spread they traversed. The Reward entity treats self-outbid identically to any other outbid: `recipient_id` happens to match the new bid's `bidder_id`.
   272→
   273→---
   274→
   275→## 7. Entity: WTABid
   276→
   277→The Winner Takes All bid is structurally different from individual bids: it covers all 52 paintings, is always lawyer-mediated, and immediately closes the auction.
   278→
   279→### 7.1 Schema
   280→
   281→| Field | Type | Description |
   282→|---|---|---|
   283→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
   284→| `bidder_id` * | → Bidder | The WTA buyer |
   285→| `total_amount_cents` * | integer | Total WTA price at time of placement (sum of next-tier-up for all 52 paintings) |
   286→| `per_painting_amounts` * | json | Array of 52 objects: `{painting_id, tier_equivalent, amount_cents}` — snapshot of each painting's contribution |
   287→| `contract_reference` * | string | Legal contract document reference |
   288→| `escrow_reference` * | string | Fiduciary's escrow tracking number |
   289→| `status` * | enum | `pending_contract`, `pending_payment`, `payment_confirmed`, `settled`, `cancelled` |
   290→| `placed_at` | datetime | When wire transfer was confirmed by fiduciary |
   291→| `created_at` * | datetime | Record creation timestamp |
   292→
   293→### 7.2 WTA Price Calculation
   294→
   295→The WTA price is a real-time computation, not a stored value:
   296→
   297→```
   298→wta_price = SUM over all 52 paintings of:
   299→  IF painting has no active bid     → tier1_price_cents
   300→  IF painting has active tier1 bid  → tier2_price_cents
   301→  IF painting has active tier2 bid  → tier3_price_cents
   302→  IF painting has active tier3 bid  → tier4_price_cents
   303→```
   304→
   305→Display updates in real time on the WTA page and homepage.
   306→
   307→### 7.3 WTA Trigger Sequence
   308→
   309→When a WTA payment is confirmed:
   310→
   311→1. **Freeze the auction.** No new bids accepted. Any `payment_processing` bids are aborted and their payments reversed.
   312→2. **Snapshot the WTA price breakdown.** Record `per_painting_amounts` — this is the binding price.
   313→3. **Refund all active bidders.** Every bid with `status = active` transitions to `refunded_wta`. Refunds initiated immediately.
   314→4. **Calculate rewards.** For each painting with an outbid history, create Reward records as if the WTA tier-equivalent were the winning bid.
   315→5. **Proceed to settlement.** Threshold check is skipped — WTA always meets the threshold.
   316→
   317→---
   318→
   319→## 8. Auction State Machine
   320→
   321→### 8.1 Painting-Level State Machine
   322→
   323→Each painting independently tracks its bid state. The painting's state is derived from its bids, not stored separately — it's a computed property.
   324→
   325→```
   326→                    ┌─────────┐
   327→                    │  Open   │
   328→                    └────┬────┘
   329→                         │ Tier 1 bid placed
   330→                         ▼
   331→                  ┌──────────────┐
   332→                  │ Tier 1 Held  │
   333→                  └──────┬───────┘
   334→                         │ Tier 2 bid placed (Tier 1 bidder refunded)
   335→                         ▼
   336→                  ┌──────────────┐
   337→                  │ Tier 2 Held  │
   338→                  └──────┬───────┘
   339→                         │ Tier 3 bid placed (Tier 2 bidder refunded)
   340→                         ▼
   341→                  ┌──────────────┐
   342→                  │ Tier 3 Held  │ ← No further individual bids possible
   343→                  └──────────────┘
   344→```
   345→
   346→**State derivation rules:**
   347→
   348→| Condition | State | Available Tier |
   349→|---|---|---|
   350→| No `active` bid exists | `open` | Tier 1 |
   351→| `active` bid at `tier1` exists | `tier1_held` | Tier 2 |
   352→| `active` bid at `tier2` exists | `tier2_held` | Tier 3 |
   353→| `active` bid at `tier3` exists | `tier3_held` | None |
   354→| `payment_processing` bid at available tier | `bid_in_progress` | None (temporarily) |
   355→
   356→**Post-auction states** (after December 31 or WTA trigger):
   357→
   358→| Condition | State |
   359→|---|---|
   360→| Auction succeeded, painting has active bid | `sold` |
   361→| Auction succeeded, painting has no bid | `unsold` |
   362→| Auction failed | `auction_failed` |
   363→| WTA triggered | `sold_wta` |
   364→
   365→### 8.2 Auction-Level State Machine
   366→
   367→The auction itself has a lifecycle:
   368→
   369→```
   370→  ┌────────────┐
   371→  │  Pre-Launch │  ← Content loading, paintings cataloged, no bidding
   372→  └─────┬──────┘
   373→        │ Principal approval (HC-04)
   374→        ▼
   375→  ┌────────────┐
   376→  │   Active   │  ← Bidding open, real-time updates
   377→  └─────┬──────┘
   378→        │
   379→        ├── December 31, 2026 reached ──► Closing
   380→        │
   381→        └── WTA bid confirmed ──► WTA Settlement
   382→
   383→  ┌────────────┐
   384→  │  Closing   │  ← Grace window: no new bids, in-flight bids may complete (5 min)
   385→  └─────┬──────┘
   386→        │ Grace window expires
   387→        ▼
   388→  ┌────────────┐
   389→  │ Evaluation │  ← Threshold check (§8.3)
   390→  └─────┬──────┘
   391→        │
   392→        ├── Threshold met ──► Settlement
   393→        │
   394→        └── Threshold not met ──► Failed
   395→
   396→  ┌────────────┐
   397→  │ Settlement │  ← Disbursements, rewards, certificates
   398→  └─────┬──────┘
   399→        │
   400→        └── All disbursements complete ──► Archive
   401→
   402→  ┌────────────┐
   403→  │   Failed   │  ← All active bids refunded, no rewards
   404→  └─────┬──────┘
   405→        │
   406→        └── All refunds complete ──► Archive
   407→
   408→  ┌──────────────┐
   409→  │ WTA Settlement│ ← WTA-specific disbursement
   410→  └──────┬────────┘
   411→         │
   412→         └── Complete ──► Archive
   413→
   414→  ┌────────────┐
   415→  │  Archive   │  ← Permanent online gallery
   416→  └────────────┘
   417→```
   418→
   419→### 8.3 Threshold Evaluation
   420→
   421→Performed exactly once, at the moment the auction close datetime is reached:
   422→
   423→```
   424→aggregate = SUM of (highest active bid amount_cents) for each painting that has at least one active bid
   425→
   426→threshold = SUM of tier1_price_cents for all 52 paintings
   427→
   428→IF aggregate >= threshold → Auction succeeds → proceed to Settlement
   429→IF aggregate < threshold → Auction fails → proceed to Failed
   430→```
   431→
   432→**Important:** Paintings with no bids contribute $0 to the aggregate, not their Tier 1 price. The threshold is the sum of all Tier 1 prices — the auction must collectively generate at least that amount.
   433→
   434→### 8.4 Settlement Sequence
   435→
   436→After threshold passes (or WTA is confirmed):
   437→
   438→1. **Freeze state.** Auction status → `settlement`. No further mutations.
   439→2. **Calculate rewards.** For each painting, walk the bid chain and create Reward records (§6.2).
   440→3. **Calculate artist disbursements.** For each painting:
   441→   - Base: Tier 1 equivalent price
   442→   - Per-spread artist share: 50% of each spread
   443→   - Total: base + sum of artist spread shares
   444→4. **Deduct fiduciary fees** from artist's total proceeds (not from bidder funds).
   445→5. **Disburse rewards** to outbid bidders via fiduciary.
   446→6. **Disburse artist proceeds** via fiduciary.
   447→7. **Generate certificates of authenticity** (digital PDF per won painting).
   448→8. **Notify all participants** (§10).
   449→9. **Transition to archive** when all disbursements confirmed.
   450→
   451→---
   452→
   453→## 9. Entity: Auction
   454→
   455→A singleton entity representing the auction itself. There is exactly one auction.
   456→
   457→### 9.1 Schema
   458→
   459→| Field | Type | Description |
   460→|---|---|---|
   461→| `id` * | uuid | Unique identifier |
   462→| `name` * | string | "The Charlotte Collection" |
   463→| `status` * | enum | `pre_launch`, `active`, `closing`, `evaluation`, `settlement`, `wta_settlement`, `failed`, `archive` |
   464→| `threshold_cents` * | integer | Sum of all 52 paintings' `tier1_price_cents`. Computed at catalog finalization. |
   465→| `open_at` * | datetime | Auction launch datetime (spring/summer 2026 — exact date TBD) |
   466→| `close_at` * | datetime | `2026-12-31T23:59:59-05:00` (Eastern Time — Charlotte, NC) |
   467→| `closed_early_at` | datetime | If WTA triggered, the datetime of early close |
   468→| `settlement_started_at` | datetime | When settlement processing began |
   469→| `settlement_completed_at` | datetime | When all disbursements were confirmed |
   470→| `created_at` * | datetime | Record creation timestamp |
   471→
   472→### 9.2 Real-Time Computed Values
   473→
   474→These are not stored — computed on demand and pushed to clients:
   475→
   476→| Value | Formula | Display Location |
   477→|---|---|---|
   478→| **Aggregate** | Sum of highest active bid per painting | Homepage, Auction page, Dashboard |
   479→| **Threshold progress %** | `(aggregate / threshold_cents) × 100` | Homepage, Auction page, Dashboard |
   480→| **Paintings with bids** | Count of paintings with ≥1 active bid | Homepage, Auction page |
   481→| **Total bidders** | Count of distinct bidders with ≥1 active bid | Homepage |
   482→| **Days remaining** | `close_at - now()` | Homepage, Auction page, Dashboard |
   483→| **WTA price** | See §7.2 | Homepage, WTA page |
   484→
   485→---
   486→
   487→## 10. Notification Events
   488→
   489→Notifications are triggered by state transitions and temporal milestones. Each event has a defined trigger, audience, channel, and content template (templates specified in 03a §15).
   490→
   491→### 10.1 Event Catalog
   492→
   493→| Event | Trigger | Audience | Channels | Template (03a ref) |
   494→|---|---|---|---|---|
   495→| `bid_confirmed` | Bid status → `active` | The bidder | Email | §15.1 |
   496→| `outbid` | Bid status → `outbid` | The outbid bidder | Email + SMS + Push (per prefs) | §15.2 |
   497→| `wta_triggered` | WTA payment confirmed | All bidders with active or outbid bids | Email + SMS + Push | §15.3 |
   498→| `milestone_threshold` | Aggregate crosses 25% / 50% / 75% of threshold | All registered bidders | Email | §15.4 |
   499→| `milestone_painting` | First painting reaches Tier 3, or bid count milestones (10, 25, 40, 52) | All registered bidders | Email | §15.4 |
   500→| `closing_warning_7d` | 7 days before close_at | All bidders with active bids | Email + SMS | §15.5 |
   501→| `closing_warning_24h` | 24 hours before close_at | All bidders with active bids | Email + SMS | §15.5 |
   502→| `closing_warning_1h` | 1 hour before close_at | All bidders with active bids | Email + SMS | §15.5 |
   503→| `auction_success` | Threshold evaluation passes | All bidders (active + outbid) | Email | §15.6 |
   504→| `auction_failed` | Threshold evaluation fails | All bidders (active + outbid) | Email | §15.7 |
   505→| `reward_disbursed` | Reward status → `disbursed` | Reward recipient | Email | §15.6 (reward variant) |
   506→| `refund_processed` | Refund confirmed by payment processor | The refunded bidder | Email | (confirmation email) |
   507→
   508→### 10.2 Channel Routing Rules
   509→
   510→| Channel | When Used | Controllable by Bidder? |
   511→|---|---|---|
   512→| **Email** | All events | No — always on (required for legal/transactional) |
   513→| **SMS** | Outbid, WTA trigger, closing warnings | Yes — opt-in toggle in Dashboard |
   514→| **Push** | Outbid, WTA trigger | Yes — opt-in toggle in Dashboard |
   515→
   516→### 10.3 Timing Constraints
   517→
   518→- `outbid` notification: sent within **60 seconds** of the outbid event. This is the highest-priority notification — the bidder needs to know immediately.
   519→- `bid_confirmed`: sent within **5 minutes** of payment confirmation.
   520→- `wta_triggered`: sent within **15 minutes** to all affected bidders.
   521→- Milestone and closing warnings: sent within **1 hour** of trigger (not real-time-critical).
   522→- `refund_processed`: sent when payment processor confirms the refund (processor-dependent timing).
   523→
   524→### 10.4 Notification Deduplication
   525→
   526→- A bidder should never receive duplicate notifications for the same event.
   527→- If a bidder is outbid on multiple paintings simultaneously (e.g., WTA trigger), send one consolidated email listing all affected paintings, not 52 separate emails.
   528→- Milestone notifications are sent at most once per milestone level (e.g., one email at 50%, not repeated if the aggregate dips and re-crosses).
   529→
   530→---
   531→
   532→## 11. Content Management
   533→
   534→Content is created by William (the artist) and loaded before auction launch. Some content is updatable during the auction.
   535→
   536→### 11.1 Painting Content
   537→
   538→| Content | Source | Updatable During Auction? |
   539→|---|---|---|
   540→| Title | Artist | No — affects slug, references |
   541→| Dimensions | Physical measurement | No — affects pricing |
   542→| Narrative text | Artist | Yes — artist can refine descriptions |
   543→| Video URL | Artist / production | Yes — new video can be added |
   544→| Images (all sizes) | Photography | Yes — better photos can replace originals |
   545→| Alt text | Derived from title + dimensions | No — auto-generated |
   546→
   547→### 11.2 Site Content
   548→
   549→| Content | Location (in site design) | Source |
   550→|---|---|---|
   551→| Charlotte Collection intro | Homepage §4.1 | Artist + editor |
   552→| Auction explanation | Auction page §5.1 | Instance (CTO) |
   553→| How Bidding Works full text | §8 | Instance + fiduciary |
   554→| Terms of Service | §8.1 Section 9 | Fiduciary lawyer |
   555→| Artist statement | About page §9.1 | Artist |
   556→| Charlotte Collection story | About page §9.1 | Artist |
   557→| Grief Series narrative | Series page §10.2 | Artist |
   558→| Press / media section | About page §9.1 | Instance |
   559→| Footer legal text | §3.2 | Instance + legal |
   560→
   561→### 11.3 Content Pipeline
   562→
   563→Content entry method is a Phase 4 decision (CMS admin interface vs. code deployment vs. headless CMS). This spec defines what content exists and its lifecycle constraints — not how it's entered.
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→## 12. Financial Summary Schema
   568→
   569→For settlement reporting, the system must produce a complete financial summary.
   570→
   571→### 12.1 Per-Painting Settlement Record
   572→
   573→| Field | Value |
   574→|---|---|
   575→| Painting | Title, dimensions, square inches |
   576→| Winning tier | Tier 1/2/3 (or "Unsold" or "WTA") |
   577→| Winning bid amount | Dollar amount |
   578→| Winning bidder | Name (legal) |
   579→| Bid chain | Ordered list of all bids placed (tier, bidder, amount, timestamp) |
   580→| Tier 1→2 spread reward | Amount, recipient |
   581→| Tier 2→3 spread reward | Amount, recipient |
   582→| Tier 3→4 spread reward (WTA only) | Amount, recipient |
   583→| Artist base (Tier 1 equivalent) | Amount |
   584→| Artist spread shares | Per-tier amounts |
   585→| Artist total for this painting | Sum of base + spread shares |
   586→
   587→### 12.2 Auction-Level Summary
   588→
   589→| Field | Value |
   590→|---|---|
   591→| Total escrow received | Sum of all winning bids |
   592→| Total rewards disbursed | Sum of all reward payments |
   593→| Total artist gross | Total escrow - total rewards |
   594→| Fiduciary fees | Amount (deducted from artist gross) |
   595→| Artist net | Artist gross - fiduciary fees |
   596→| Total paintings sold | Count |
   597→| Total paintings unsold | Count |
   598→| Total unique bidders | Count |
   599→| Total bids placed | Count (including outbid bids) |
   600→
   601→### 12.3 Tax Reporting
   602→
   603→- **Rewards** may be taxable income. The fiduciary may need to issue **1099-MISC** forms to reward recipients receiving $600+ in a calendar year.
   604→- **Sales tax** applies per the buyer's state (Wayfair). Rate determination and collection is a Phase 4 decision (likely handled by the payment processor or a tax service like TaxJar/Avalara).
   605→- **Artist income** reported as business revenue. The fiduciary maintains records per HC-06.
   606→
   607→---
   608→
   609→## 13. Temporal Rules
   610→
   611→### 13.1 Auction Lifecycle Timeline
   612→
   613→| Phase | Start | End | What's Possible |
   614→|---|---|---|---|
   615→| **Pre-launch** | Catalog creation | Principal approval (HC-04) | Content loading, painting CRUD, no bidding |
   616→| **Active** | Auction open | Dec 31 2026 23:59:59 ET (or WTA trigger) | Bidding, content updates, real-time data |
   617→| **Evaluation** | Close datetime reached | Threshold check complete | No mutations — read-only moment |
   618→| **Settlement / Failed** | After evaluation | All disbursements / refunds complete | Financial processing only |
   619→| **Archive** | After settlement | Forever | Read-only permanent gallery |
   620→
   621→### 13.2 Immutability Schedule
   622→
   623→| Data | Becomes Immutable At |
   624→|---|---|
   625→| Painting catalog (titles, dimensions, pricing) | Auction open |
   626→| Gallery display order | Auction open |
   627→| Bid records | Immediately on creation (append-only — status transitions are the only mutations) |
   628→| Reward calculations | Settlement initiation |
   629→| Terms of service | Auction open (HC-03) |
   630→
   631→### 13.3 Close Datetime
   632→
   633→`2026-12-31T23:59:59-05:00` (Eastern Time, Charlotte NC). Fixed. No extensions (Key Decision #6, HC-03). The system must handle:
   634→
   635→- Bids submitted in the final seconds (any bid where `payment_processing` was created before the deadline and payment confirms within a grace window — recommended: 5 minutes — is accepted)
   636→- Time zone display for bidders outside Eastern Time
   637→- Leap second edge cases (treat as non-issue; standard datetime libraries handle this)
   638→
   639→---
   640→
   641→## 14. Phase 3 Open Question Resolutions (Data/State)
   642→
   643→| ID | Question | Resolution |
   644→|---|---|---|
   645→| J02e-03 | Show reward dollar amount in dashboard before settlement? | **No.** Dashboard shows "Reward at settlement if auction succeeds." Amount revealed only at settlement. Matches 03a §11.1 and §15.2. |
   646→
   647→### Still Pending (External Dependencies)
   648→
   649→| ID | Question | Impact on Data/State | Owner |
   650→|---|---|---|---|
   651→| AM-OQ-01 | Securities counsel review | May affect reward display rules, terms language | William |
   652→| AM-OQ-02 | Fiduciary lawyer selection | Escrow reference formats, settlement API integration | William |
   653→| AM-OQ-04 | Painting inventory (52 with dimensions) | Populates all Painting records, sets threshold_cents | William |
   654→| J02a-08 | Wire transfer threshold | Determines which tier buttons trigger credit card vs. lawyer-mediated flow | Fiduciary |
   655→| J02c-01 | Credit card / lawyer-mediated threshold | Same — affects bid flow routing logic | Fiduciary |
   656→
   657→---
   658→
   659→## 15. Cross-Reference: Site Design ↔ Data/State
   660→
   661→This table maps every dynamic element in 03a to the data entity and computation that powers it.
   662→
   663→| Site Design Element (03a ref) | Data Source | Computation |
   664→|---|---|---|
   665→| Gallery scroller bid status (§12.2) | Painting state (§8.1) | Derived from active bids |
   666→| Tier button states (§13.2) | Painting state + Bid.status | Available/Held/InProgress/Grayed |
   667→| Tier button prices (§13.3) | Painting.tier{N}_price_cents | Direct lookup |
   668→| Current bidder name (§6.1) | Bid.display_name + Bid.visibility | Show name if public, "Private Bidder" if private |
   669→| Threshold progress bar (§4.1) | Auction computed values (§9.2) | aggregate / threshold_cents |
   670→| WTA price (§7.2) | WTA calculation (§7.2) | Sum of next-tier-up per painting |
   671→| Days remaining (§4.1) | Auction.close_at | close_at - now() |
   672→| Paintings with bids count (§4.1) | Bid entity | Count distinct painting_id where status = active |
   673→| Dashboard active bids (§11.1) | Bid entity | Where bidder_id = current_user AND status = active |
   674→| Dashboard outbid history (§11.1) | Bid entity + Reward entity | Where bidder_id = current_user AND status = outbid |
   675→| Shipping estimate (§6.3) | Painting.size_category | Lookup from size_category → estimate range |
   676→| Notification templates (§15.*) | Notification events (§10) | Template populated from Bid, Painting, Bidder, Reward entities |
   677→| Open Graph dynamic descriptions (§16.3) | Painting state | Server-rendered from current bid status |
   678→

Based on the search results, here's information about Truist Financial Corporation's small business banking accounts, fees, and minimums for 2026:

## Account Types & Minimum Opening Deposit

Truist requires a $100 minimum opening deposit for small business accounts. Truist offers six small-business checking accounts and two business savings accounts.

## Key Account Options

**Simple Business Checking:**
Truist offers a free business checking account in Truist Simple Business Checking, where you don't pay any monthly maintenance fee and get some free transactions and cash deposits each month. This account allows up to 50 monthly transactions and $2,000 monthly cash processing at no charge.

**Business Savings Accounts:**
For Simple Business Savings, you keep a $250 minimum daily balance, a $500 average monthly balance, or set up a recurring deposit of at least $100 each month. Business Money Market just requires you to keep a $5,000 minimum daily balance.

## Fee Schedule Updates

Price changes are effective January 1, 2026, and specific to Truist's various Packaged and Small Business Banking Account types, with these fees reflected on your statement as Account Analysis fees beginning with your January 2026 statement.

For more detailed information about specific fees and all account options, you can access Truist's official Business Deposit Accounts Fee Schedule effective January 1, 2026.

1→# FHOA Instance Constraints
     2→
     3→*Layer 1 — C3PO-owned, read-only in subsidiary*
     4→*Source: ctrl-instance-governance-standard.md*
     5→*Authored: 2026-03-15*
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Purpose
    10→
    11→Non-negotiable rules that Fine Higher Ordered Art must obey. These constraints derive from intellectual property law, consumer protection law, tax law, enterprise policy, and the nature of original artwork as a unique physical asset. FHOA may not waive or modify these constraints — changes require a C3PO amendment exchange.
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→## Hard Constraints
    16→
    17→### HC-01: Artist Retains Intellectual Property
    18→
    19→The Principal retains all intellectual property rights to every original painting. Sale of a physical painting transfers ownership of the physical object only — not reproduction rights, digital rights, or derivative work rights. No reproduction, print, or digital copy of any artwork may be created or distributed without explicit written authorization from the Principal.
    20→
    21→### HC-02: Accurate Representation of Artwork
    22→
    23→All artwork listed for sale must be accurately represented. Product listings must include accurate photographs, dimensions, medium, and condition. No digital manipulation that misrepresents the appearance of the physical work. Buyers must receive what they saw.
    24→
    25→### HC-03: Auction Terms Disclosed Before Bidding
    26→
    27→All auction terms — including bid tiers, timing, payment terms, shipping costs, return policy, and any reserve — must be clearly disclosed to bidders before the auction opens. No term changes after bidding begins. The auction mechanism must be transparent.
    28→
    29→### HC-04: Human Approval for Irreversible Financial Commitments
    30→
    31→The Principal must approve before: launching an auction, accepting a final bid, initiating payment processing setup, or entering any contract with a payment processor, shipping provider, or gallery. AI agents may prepare decision packages but may not authorize commitments.
    32→
    33→### HC-05: Customer Data Protection
    34→
    35→Customer personal and financial data must be encrypted at rest and in transit. Payment processing must use a PCI-compliant third-party processor — FHOA must not store credit card numbers, bank account details, or payment credentials directly. Customer contact information may be retained for relationship management but must not be sold or shared.
    36→
    37→### HC-06: Tax Compliance
    38→
    39→All sales must comply with applicable sales tax requirements (state-by-state for online sales per *South Dakota v. Wayfair*). Income from art sales must be reported per IRS requirements. Art-specific tax considerations (hobby vs. business, material deductions, home studio deduction) must be handled correctly. CFO approval gate applies to all tax posture decisions.
    40→
    41→### HC-07: Art-Safe Shipping and Handling
    42→
    43→Physical artwork must be shipped using art-appropriate packaging (rigid corners, acid-free wrapping, insured shipment). Shipping insurance must cover replacement value. The Principal must approve the shipping method and carrier for each sale until a standard process is established and validated.
    44→
    45→### HC-08: Secrets and Credentials Security
    46→
    47→API keys, payment processor credentials, and any authentication secrets must never appear in application code, version control, logs, or any artifact accessible without explicit Principal authorization. Use environment variables or a secret management service.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Truist's treasury payment solutions for small businesses:

## Overview
Truist offers commercial treasury and cash flow management solutions to help manage payables, receivables, liquidity, and more. The Small Business Administration consistently ranks Truist as one of the top small-business-friendly banks in the nation.

## Key Treasury Solutions
Truist is helping businesses turn payments into a competitive advantage, from fighting fraud to unlocking real time insights and reducing costs through automation. Solutions focus on three key areas: simplicity, streamlining operations by replacing legacy systems; speed, accelerating access to insights and capital; and safety, strengthening fraud defenses with smarter tech.

## Specific Tools and Services
- **Truist One View**: This allows you to use and administer treasury accounts by accessing multiple banking applications all in one place.

- **Payment Solutions**: Automate and streamline payments through innovative, secure, web-based solutions.

Truist introduced its first open banking integration in February 2026, connecting first to Mastercard's open finance technology. ## API and Security Standards

The agreement moves Truist's Open Banking infrastructure away from credential-sharing towards an API-based model aligned with the Financial Data Exchange (FDX) standard. Truist's mid-term strategy includes launching an API marketplace, allowing third-party developers to create innovative financial products on its platform. Truist is actively pursuing fintech partnerships, with plans to launch an innovation lab for co-creation with startups.

# Stripe and Truist Relationship Analysis

Based on the podcast transcript, the relationship between Stripe and Truist centers on **fintech-bank partnership dynamics** rather than a direct integration or formal collaboration.

## Nature of Collaboration

The discussion frames Stripe and Truist as representatives of two distinct ecosystem roles:

- **Stripe's Position**: As a fintech/payments company working with multiple partner banks
- **Truist's Position**: As a large regional bank managing relationships with fintech partners

## Key Collaboration Principles

Harsha Ragunath (Stripe) emphasizes proactive engagement:

> "Having those conversations early on in the process before we've even signed, or have got to a term sheet, to understand, are our risk tolerances aligned?"

Babette Reynolds (Truist) stresses mutual responsibility:

> "Take the bank's regulatory requirements seriously...have those discussions early on about what the risk and compliance requirements are going to be."

## Compliance Partnership Focus

The discussion highlights best practices rather than specific integrations:

1. **Early-stage dialogue** on regulatory expectations
2. **Ongoing monitoring** post-launch
3. **Risk alignment** before formal agreements
4. **Transparent issue reporting** between parties

## Strategic Takeaway

Both speakers advocate embedding compliance into product development from inception, with fintechs establishing dedicated compliance functions to facilitate bank partnerships—positioning compliance as a bridge rather than a barrier to collaboration.

**Merchant Services:** Truist serves 55k+ clients, $22B+ volume, top-20 acquirer. Backend is Global Payments/TSYS. E-commerce path is through Authorize.net (Truist is a reseller). Integrates with Shopify, WooCommerce, custom platforms. Next-business-day funding to Truist checking.

**Stripe Relationship:** No direct integration or partnership. Compliance dialogue only. Truist's merchant processing runs through Global Payments/TSYS + Authorize.net. Stripe works independently with any bank account.

**APIs:** No public developer API. Open banking launched Feb 2026 (Mastercard) and expanded Mar 2026 (Plaid) — consumer-facing data access, not platform integration. API marketplace planned but not launched.

**Geographic Footprint:** 17 states + DC. 1,900+ branches. Strongest in FL, NC, VA, GA, PA.

**Arts Programs:** None at banking level. Foundation supports creative nonprofits. Wealth Sports & Entertainment group requires $1M+.

**Reputation:** 1.1/5 Trustpilot. Below-average J.D. Power. Post-merger workforce down 36% (59k→38k). $240M overdraft settlement. Ongoing restructuring through 2026.

**Recommendation architecture for FHOA:** Truist Dynamic Business Checking as the bank account, Stripe for payment processing (developer-first, superior API for custom platform), self-managed escrow or Stripe manual capture for auction holds. input.content:
# Fiduciary Attorney Research — Heather R. Klein

**Date:** 2026-03-26
**Purpose:** Evaluate Heather R. Klein as potential fiduciary attorney for the Charlotte Collection auction.

---

## Why FHOA Needs a Fiduciary Attorney

The auction's financial architecture has three layers:

1. **Stripe** — processes credit card payments, handles PCI compliance, issues refunds
2. **Fiduciary attorney** — holds all funds in trust, executes disbursements, mediates high-value transactions
3. **Bank** — operating account where Stripe payouts land and expenses are paid

The fiduciary attorney is the critical layer. A bank cannot replace this role — bank escrow/trust services are institutional-scale (M&A, municipal bonds) and wrong-sized for this auction. The fiduciary needs to:

- Hold all auction funds in an IOLTA or dedicated trust account
- Confirm wire receipt for high-value bids and communicate status to the platform
- Execute immediate refunds when bidders are outbid
- Calculate and disburse outbid rewards (50/50 spread split) at settlement
- Reconcile settlement across up to 52 paintings with variable tier chains
